sutta: the buddha said monks there are these 6 ways of being considerate which endear bring honor lead to concord banish contention lead to unity lead to singleness of mind what's 6 here in monks amongst part is good will indeed towards his fellows in the holy life openly and in private verily this is a way of being considerate again his part is good will in word good will in thought towards his fellows in the holy life openly and in private this also is a way of being considerate then those gains gotten according to rule be they but bold scraps he loves to share them impartially to have them in common with his virtuous fellows in the holy life this also is a way of being considerate and those virtues that are unbroken without flaw spotless without blemish bringing freedom praised by wise men incorruptible leading to concentration he dwells 1 in virtue with them among his fellows in the holy life openly and in private this also is a way of being considerate and that aryan view saving leading him who acts accordingly to the utter destruction of dukkha or he dwells 1 in view with that among his fellows in the holy life openly and in private this also is a way of being considerate verily monks these are the 6 ways of being considerate which endear bring honor lead to concord banish contention lead to unity lead to singleness of mind it's the end of the suta
commentary: this suta but with uh it's also mentioned in the diga nikaya number 16 and the buddha there said that there are certain conditions like if a group of people or a race etc are to be harmonious or to be strong there are certain things that they should practice and in particular for monks are important the first 1 if monks live together they should have good will towards each other indeed in their action whether openly or in private they should show metha goodwill in their actions towards the other people in the monastery that they live with the second 1 they should have meta or goodwill in their words openly and in private towards the other monks who live in the community in the monastery third 1 is good will in thought towards his fellow monks in the monastery whether openly or in private he should have made time and the fourth 1 those uh gains and those offerings that he receives [Music] those extras and he receives he should share them impartially with his fellow monks in the holy life sometimes even if it's not excess sometimes he sees that other monks don't have it is good to share because this method if we show meta towards others we receive it back in return and the fifth fifth 1 is the virtues like moral conduct he should practice them so that he gains the respect and love of his fellows companions in the holy life yeah and then the right view right view also he should attain so that he can dwell in concord and harmony with his companions in the holy life and the right viewer we heard earlier to attain right view we must be very familiar with the discourses of the buddha and then only we can attain the right view the nexuta

sutta: on 1 occasion while he dwelt near savarti and jeter grove in anatha pindika's park the exalted 1 rising from solitude in the evening approached the service hall of salah and on arriving sat down on the seat ready there then too the wearable sariputta rising from solitude in the evening went to the service hall saluted the exalted 1 and sat down at 1 side so like boys came the venerable maha mogalana and the venable ananda and they all sat down at 1 side now after passing much of the night thus seated the exalted 1 rose and entered the dwelling and soon after he had gone dependable monks also rose and and went each to his dwelling but the novices not long gone forth newly come to this dhamma vinaya went on snoring and sleeping there even until sunrise i just stopped here for a moment you see normally during the buddha's time from the sutasana we find that the monks in the morning they go out on pin the bat arms around and then they come back and they take the meal and after taking the meal they would find a secluded place to practice meditation and this they would do for the most part of the day and then when the sun goes down they would go to where the leader was in this case the buddha was there they would go to the buddha if the buddha was not around then they would go to the most senior monk and there they would discuss dhamma uh sometimes they'll discuss dharma and like in this case this 1 of the days when the buddha had nothing to say to them probably he actually taught them so much so he just sat in meditation and they also followed him said in meditation and after some time he went back to his good tea to take his rest and senior monks also did like wisely went back to the couti to take a rest but the young monks are they were in the salah and they were sleeping in the salah and snoring there until the sunrise and the suta continues and the exalted 1 seeing with the deva eye surpassing the purity of mans those monks snoring and sleeping till sunrise went to the service hall and sat down and being seated addressed them saying monks where is sariputta where is maha mogalana where is mahakasapa maha kachana mahakotita mahachunda maha kapina anuruda where have these elder disciples gone monks and they answered lord day 2 soon after the exalted 1's departure rose and went each to his dwelling and the buddha said monks then you are now elders yet novice like you sleep and snore till sunrise what thank you monks have you either seen or heard of a warrior raja crowned and anointed living a life of indulgence giving given over to the pleasures of the bed indulance and sloth to his heart's content and ruling all his life the darling and favorite of the country for no indeed lord well monks neither have i or have you seen or heard of a farmer a land owner son a general a village hit man a guild master each living a life of indulgence and each managing his affairs all his life the darling and favorite of his underlings no indeed lord well monks neither have i then have you ever seen or heard of a recluse or a brahmin indulging himself to his heart's content in the pleasures of bed indolence and sloth which sends doors unguarded knowing no moderation in eating not practicing wakefulness without right view nor practicing the practice day in and day out of making become things that wing to his awakening yet having destroyed the asavas and being asava free entering and abiding in liberation by mind in liberation by wisdom and realizing it here and now by his own knowledge no indeed lord well monks neither have i therefore monks train yourselves thus we will become guarded as to the sense doors no moderation in eating we practice in wakefulness possess right view and dwell day in and day out practicing the practice of making become things that wing to our awakening verily monk strain yourselves thus that's the end of the sutta
commentary: here the buddha is telling the the young monks and that when the elder monks are not around and they are considered the senior monks then they should act like senior monks and not indulge in sleep unmindfully you can see here that the buddha's standard is quite high and he doesn't like his disciples to sleep so much in the buddha's teachings he encouraged the disciples to sleep just a minimum amount just enough to have enough wrestler and these things that he encouraged the young monks to practice these are the important practices guarded as to the sense doors moderation and eating devoted to wakefulness possessed right view etc so i'll stop here and if you have any questions we can discuss them suta

winning truth and realizing nibana i mean going to see the tata gata or his disciple endowed with faith and piety sure in trust this month is called the sight above all hearing is the sight above all sound above all hearing is the sight and sound above all and what monks is again above all here in monks some gain the gain of a son or a wife or wealth gain diverse things gain faith in some recluse mind the gain of a son or a wife or wealth ever mind diverse gains ever mind some recluse or brahmin wrong in view treading the wrong path and is that the ever-minding monks no i say it is not it is realizing nibana i mean ever minding that tagatha or his disciple endowed with faith and piety sure in trust this month is called the ever-minding above all verily amongst these are the sikhs above commentary:
here the buddha is saying that the the site above all sides is going to see that or his disciple and then the sound above all sounds is to hear that the tagatha's dharma all his disciples and the gain above all gains is to gain faith in the tatar gatherer of his disciple and the training above all is the training in the higher virtue the higher mind and the higher wisdom is the adi sila adichitta and adipanya and then the the service above all services is to serve the tata gata or his disciple and the ever-minding above allah that is remembering is to have a minor that the tagatha or the tattagatas disciple you see the buddha is saying the right path leads to world weariness dispassion ending calm knowledge awakening nibana if we practice anything that leads to greater passion for the world and greater excitement for the world that is not the spiritual path not the holy path it is a path that leads to enjoyment of the world which ends in more dukkha but the path that leads to weariness of the world is a part that leads to a dispassion for the world and that is a path that leads up ultimately to the greatest happiness

sutta: monks these 6 are the roots of contention what's 6 monks suppose among the angry scornful and he being so he lives without reverence with respect for the master for dhamma for the sangha and he fulfills not the training and he who lives thus causes contention in the order and that is to the hurt and sorrow of many folk to the harm of many folk to the hurt and ill of devas and men monks if you see this root of contention among you or among others strive to rid yourselves of this evil thing and if you see it not stepped away to stop its future cankering or arising thus is this evil root of contention got rid of and thus there shall be no more future cankering or arising again monk suppose among be a hypocrite malicious or envious mean or deceitful crafty or evil minded and wrong in view of you bound tenacious stubborn and if he be any of these things he lives without reverence respect for the master and dhamma and sangha norfolk will see the training and he who lives thus causes contention in the sangha to the hurt and ill of devas and men monks if you see these rules of contention strive to be rid of these evil things and if you see them not stepped away to stop its future gankering or arising thus are these evil roots rid and thus there shall be no future cankering or arising early monks these are the 6 roots of contention that's the end of the suta
commentary: here the buddha is saying that there are certain qualities that if among possesses it leads to contention um these are unwholesome traits or habits like the first 1 is being angry or scornful the second 1 is being a hypocrite not malicious that 1 envious and mean and then deceitful and crafty and then evil minded and having wrong view and then view bound that means having too much views and being stubborn and tenacious and it is not easier for people to live together for example in a monastery and there are some people who are unaccommodating and not willing to change their bad habits that such people cannot live in a monastery with others because if you allow them to live with others they will cause contention and fights and quarrels so such people if they are not willing to change their habits not willing to accommodate other people allow for other people's faults etc then it is better that they live alone and not all people can live under the same roof with others the nexu time

sutta: is also quite a good important suture once when the exalted 1 dwelt near savarti as theatre growth disciple founded an offering 6-4 and out for the sangha of monks with sariputta and maha mogalana at the head now the exalted 1 with the deva eye surpassing in clarity the eye of man saw this and addressed amongst us monks this lay disciple of velu kandaka nanda's [Music] has founded an offering sixth full and doubt for the sangha and how monks is the offering 6-fold and doubt monks the giver's part is 3 4 and the receiver's part is threefold and 1 is the giver's threefold part here in monks before the gift he is glad at heart in giving the heart is satisfied and uplifted is the heart when he has given this is the giver's threefold part and what is the receiver's 3-fold part here in monks they are lust free or stepping to cast lust out i hate free or stepping to cast hate out a delusion free or stepping to become so this is the receiver's 3-fold part thus the giver's part is 3-fold and the receiver's part is 3-fold thus verily monks the offering is 6-fold and doubt monks not easy is it to grasp the measure of merit of such a 6-fold and doubt offering and to say thus much is the yield in marriage the yield in goodness making for a lucky hereafter ripening to happiness leading heavenward leading to wheel and happiness long form loved and lovely verily this great mass of merit is reckoned just unreckonable immeasurable monks as it is not easy to grasp the measure of water in the great ocean and to say there are so many pale fools so many hundreds of peoples so many thousands of pale fools so many hundreds of thousands of pale fools for that great mass of water is reckoned unreckonable immeasurable even small monks it is not easy to grasp the measure of merit in a 6-fold endowed offering verily the great mass of merit is reckoned unrecognable immeasurable that's the end of the suta
commentary: so in this utah we find 1 day the nanda mata nanda's mother was making an offering dhana of food to and the sangha of monks and from far away the buddha because of his psychic power he saw this and he told his monks that this offering that nanda's mother was making has 6 qualities and because of these 6 qualities and the merit is immeasurable first the giver's partner the giver before he gives the offering he is very happy he or she is very happy and while he is he or she is giving the dhana he or she is very happy and even after the offering that person is again very happy that means he gives with a lot of happiness in giving without holding back and then the receiver is either a person who has um abandon a cut off lust hatred and delusion or a person walking the path to cut off lust hatred and delusion in other words that person is either an arahant or an arya who is practicing to totally eliminate lust hatred and delusion this sometimes these 3 things sometimes it's called last hatred and delusion sometimes last year would be raga hatred would be dosa and delusion would be moha sometimes these 3 things uh slight there's a slight variation sometimes it is uh uh greed hatred and delusion um greed is loba so here is mentioned mention so if a person gives a to an arya and that person gives with a lot of happiness before the offering during the offering and after the offering and then that kind of dhana is immeasurable married just as the ocean cannot be measured so to the married from this offering cannot be very um measured suit number

sutta: that's have i heard once when the venerable sariputta dwelled on mount valjoup near rajagaha he roped himself early in the morning and taking bowl and rope descended the hill with many monks in company now at a certain spot the wearable sariputta saw a large log of wood and there he addressed the monks and said see you reverence that large log of wood yes sir they replied an adapt among references 1 to mind control and if he wish view it as earth wherefore there is in that log of wood the earth element hence an adapt can view it as earth he can if he wish view it as water as fire as wind as beautiful as ugly wherefore there are references the elements of all these things in that log of wood hence an adapt 1 to mind control can view that long in such ways it's the end of the sutta
commentary: here an adapter means 1 who has finished his work meaning an arahant and the thing the pali word is edema edema is 1 who has been you could say successful because eddie 1 meaning of ed success another 1 is psychic powers so you could say 1 with psychic power or 1 with um who who has been successful in the spiritual partner but the verbal sarikuta even though he has attained all the janas he is known not to have psychic power so probably he does not refer to psychic power here he refers to an arahant 1 who has been successful in the holy path so he said no and and adapt manga and arahan can view even a lot as earth or water or fire that is heat or wind these are the 4 elements in a physical object and as beautiful or ugly this chosen that a person who has full control of his mind can do these things and actually that is why the 1 pointedness of mind now is very important samadhi in the buddha's teachings has 1 pointedness of mind is the aim of samadhi is to have control over the mind and so that as in 1 of the earlier sutas now uretna then a person can view something that is attractive as lonesome something as lonesome as even attractive and that is all because this all all these are perceptions of the mind perceptions are due to habit and perceptions can be changed if we train the mind sometimes people say that mindfulness is very important mindfulness is important but by itself it is not sufficient for example sometimes a person can know he is mindful he knows that what he is doing is wrong and yet he continues to do it why because he is not in full control of his mind for example sometimes a man or a woman may know that to commit adultery is wrong and vietnam because passion or lust controls his mind he is not he's unable to um to overcome this passion or lust so the loss of passion controls his mind rather than the mind controlling passion hence the importance of samadhi in the buddha's teachings

sutta: 1 morning the venerable ananda robing himself early took bowl and rope and went to the house of the lay disciple migasala and there sat down on a seat mate ready and meegasala came and saluted him and sat down at 1 side so seated she said to the venerable ananda pray raven sir how ought 1 to understand this dharma taught by the exalted 1 that both he who lives the holy life and he who does not shall become light wayfarers in the world to come my father sir purana lived the holy life dwelling apart abstaining from common carnal things and when he died the exalted 1 explained he is a once returner dwelling into sita heaven my uncle sir isidata did not live the holy life but rejoiced with a wife and of him also when dead the exalted 1 said he is a once returner dwelling into sita heaven reverend ananda how ought 1 to understand this dhamma and when bernanda said even sister as the exalted 1 has said and when the vulnerable ananda had received arms in me gasala's house he rose from his seat and departed now stop here for a while just to commend that probably ananda member ananda could not explain to this amiga salah that's why he said you just remember it as the exalted 1 said and now the suta continues now on his return from arms gathering after his meal the verbal ananda visited the exalted 1 saluted him and sat down at 1 side and so seated he told the exalted 1 all that had occurred adding i told her lord to understand the matter even as the exalted 1 had had explained and the buddha said but ananda who is the lay disciple of mikasala a foolish frail motherly body with but mother with to understand the diversity in the person of man these 6 persons ananda are found in the world what 6 consider ananda 1 well restrained a comely person in whose company his fellows in the holy life take pleasure yet in whom hearing dhamma is of no effect much learning is of no effect in whom there is no view penetration who wins not partial release he on the breaking up of the body after death sets out to fall not to excel fears to afford fears not to excellence consider another well restrained likewise but in whom hearing dhamma has effect much learning has effect in whom there is view penetration who wins partial release he after death sets out to excel not to fall fares to excellence not to a fall and the measurers measure them saying his stature is just this the others just that in what way is 1 wanting 1 exalted and that measuring ananda is to the measurers harm and hurt for many a day now the 1 in whom listening has effect who wins partial release that person ananda has marched further forward is more exalted than the former and why is that the stream of dhamma carries him forward ananda but who saved the tatar gather can judge that difference therefore ananda be no measurer of persons measure not the measure of persons fairly ananda he digs a bit for himself who measures the measure of persons i alone ananda can measure their measure or 1 like me consider ananda a person in whom wrath and pride are conquered but in whom greed from time to time surges in whom hearing dhamma is of no effect and another in whom hearing has effect he after death sets out to excel not to fall fears to excellence not to afford that person has much further forward is more exalted than the former i alone ananda can measure their measure consider 1 in whom wrath and pride are conquered but in whom the world of words from time to time surges in whom hearing dhamma and learning are of no effect in whom there is no view penetration who wins not partial release he after death sets out to fall not to excel fears to afford fares not to excellence and another likewise but in whom hearing dhamma and learning have effect in whom there is view penetration who wins partial release he after death sets out to excel not to fall fast to excellence not to a fall and the measurers measure them likewise and it is through their harm and hurt for many a day and in whom hearing dharma and learning have effect that 1 has much further forward is more exalted than the former and why the dhamma stream carries him forward but who saved the tatar gather can judge this difference therefore ananda be be no measurer of persons measure not the measure of persons barely ananda he digs a pit for himself who measures the measure of persons i alone ananda can measure their measure or 1 like me and who is the lay disciple me gasala a foolish frail motherly body with but mother with to understand the diversity in the person of man fairly ananda these 6 persons are found in the world ananda with such virtue as purana was endowed isidata may become endowed hearing purana fears not isidata's way but another's with such insight as isidata was endowed purana may become a doubt hearing easy data fears not purana's way but another's thus verily ananda both these men are wanting in 1 thing that's the end of the suta
commentary:
here this lady meega salah she was a bit perplexed because the father and the uncle both when they died the buddha said that they were both once returners that means sakata garmin second stage uh fruition person but 1 of them was a layperson the other 1 was a monk so this might allah couldn't understand why 1 is a lay person 1 is a monk but yet both of them obtain the same same fruit of the holy life so the buddha said explain the 6 types of persons found in the world and the buddha said not to measure another person in the buddha be measured be no measure of persons measure not the measure of persons very ananda he digs a pit for himself who measures the measure of persons so here the buddha is giving a warning that it is dangerous to judge other people especially a person who has the rope it's very difficult for lay people to judge so we have to remember to be very careful then when we criticize some monks because sometimes you might think a monk has a wrong view and he may have right view especially if among practices and keeps the precepts and meditates and you have to be very very careful and not to criticize such a manga or develop hatred for such a manga uh in the end the buddha said now that purana was although both of them purana and ec data were once returners but purana excelled in virtue whereas easy data excelled in inside so so both of them they had slightly different qualities but both of them are sakataka means

sutta: the buddha said monks is poverty a woeful thing for an immoral worldly person surely lord among replied and when among and when a man is poor needy in streets he gets into death is that woeful too surely lord and when he gets into death he borrows is that woeful too surely lord and when the bill falls due he pays not and they press him is that woeful too surely lord and when pressed he pays not and they beset him is that woeful too surely lord and when be said he pays not and they bind him tie him up is that woeful too surely lord thus monks poverty that borrowing being pressed beset and bound are all world wars for an immoral worldly person monks it is just the same for anyone who has no faith in wholesome states or skillful states is not conscientious about wholesome states has no fear of blame about wholesome states no energy for wholesome states no insight into wholesome states he is said to be poor needy in straits in the aryan discipline now that very man poor needy in straits wanting in faith conscientiousness fear of blame energy and insight concerning wholesome states works evilly in deep word and thought i call that his getting into death and to cloak his evil deeds he lays hold upon false hope let none know this of me he hopes let none know this of me he resolves let none know this of me he says let none know this of me he strives in act so likewise cloak his evil words his evil thoughts i call that his borrowing then his pious fellows in the holy life saith us this venerable sir acts in this sword carries on in this way i call that his being pressed then gone to forest tree root or lonely place evil and wholesome thoughts and attendant remorse pursue him i call that his being beset and that man monks poor and needy in straits having worked evilly deed word and thought on the breaking up of the body after death is bound in hell's bones or the bones of some animal's womb and i see no other single bondage monks so harsh so bitter such a bar to winning the unsurpassed peace from effort i mean hell's bonds or the bonds of an animal's womb that's the end of the suta

commentary: there is another suit which shows the wisdom of verbal ananda and at that time the verbal ananda was only a sotapanna while the variables sariputta was already in arahan and yet still has 1 or 2 things that he could teach to an arahant and it's not that the variable suta is less wise because the vulnerable sarikuta is said to be the highest in wisdom but the as we learned in 1 of the earlier sutras the buddha said it is hard to find somebody equal in wisdom to the rebel ananda the verb ananda [Music] as far as the the most learned as far as the sutas the teachings of the buddha um are concerned he could memorize um he could remember all the sutras taught by the buddha and this utah there are 6 things that are said to be good for a monk to practice first to master dhamma to teach others dharma in detail to make others say it in detail to make others repeat it in detail to ever ponder over it and to spend vasa where there are learned monks in executive

sutta: then another brahmin visited the exalted 1 greeted him exchanged compliments and sat down at 1 side so seated he said master gotama is there 1 thing which when made become made to increase embraces and establishes 2 wheels wheel here and wheel hereafter there is brahman what is it master gotama verily earnestness brahmin is the 1 thing which when made become made to increase embraces and establishes 2 wheels we'll hear and we'll hereafter brahmin as the footmarks of all prowling creatures are admitted in an elephants and the elephant's foot is counted chief so earnestness is the 1 thing which when made become made to increase embraces and establishes 2 wheels we'll here and we'll hereafter as all rafters in a peaked house reach to the p slope to the p unite in the peak and the peak is counter chief so earnestness brahmin is the 1 thing which when made become made to increase and embraces and establishes 2 wheels we'll hear and we'll hereafter as the light of the stars is not worth a 16th part the light of the moon and the light of the moon is counter-chief so earnestness brahmin is the 1 thing which 1 may become made to increase embraces and establishes 2 wheels we'll hear and we'll hereafter as the end of the sutta
commentary: this word earnestness is the bollywood apamada sometimes translated as diligence or sincerity and it means doing what needs to be done in the spiritual path and 1 where 1 has to be careful with half measures that is compromising um that means not being um earnest or sincere enough for example some persons want to practice the holy life and also want to get married and if they do get married and this shows a lack of earnestness and here that person ends up being neither um successful in this spiritual path or neither does he become a good husband or a good father if we are earnest or sincere we obtain happiness in this life and happiness hereafter and we should note that when we practice the spiritual path correctly and we should be happy not sad now there's some people who think that um because they are contemplating dukkha all the time they they shouldn't be um be full of dukkha but in the damage came to visit the buddha and kissed his feet and this is an indian customer which you find also in sri lanka where lay people kiss the monk's feet and the buddha said and then why ask the king why did he pay so much homage to this body and the king started to praise the buddha all the qualities of the buddha and also the monks under the buddha and the king said that whereas in other external ascetics now you could see that they were lean and wretched looking looking very miserable and unhappy whereas the monks under the buddha they looked very happy and exceedingly happy and joyful and so [Music] we should know that if we practice the holy path and we obtain the bliss of renunciation the happiness of renunciation we should be happy and looking happy and the next utah

sutta: 6 now at that time the vermont pagoona was sick ailing very ill and the bumble ananda went to the exalted 1 saluted and sat down at 1 side so seated he said to the exalted 1 lord the verbal pagana is sick healing very ill good worried lord if the exalted 1 were to go and see the wearable faguna out of compassion and the exalted 1 consented by silence then in the evening after he had come from solitude the exalted 1 visited the venerable paguna and the member of haguna saw him coming when he was some way of and stirred on his bed but the exalted 1 spoke to him and said enough aguna stir not on your bed are there not these seats here prepared already i will sit here and he sat down on the seat prepared i just stopped here for a moment this uh this baguna although he was very sick now when he saw the buddha coming and he tried to get up on his bed and these and other sutas show and that the buddha's disciples had a lot of love in addition to respect for the buddha and the suta continues so seated the exalted 1 said i hope bhaguna you are bearing up keeping going that your aches and pains grow less not more that there are signs of the growing less not more lord i can neither bear up nor keep going my aches and pains grow grievously more not less and there are signs of their growing more not less lord the violent egg that wrecks my head is just as though some lusty fellow chopped at it with a sharp edged sword lord i can neither bear up nor keep going my pains grow more not less and there are signs of the growing more not less lord the violent pain in my head is just as though some lusty fellow clapped a stout leather strap about it lord i cannot bear lord the violent step that shoots through my stomach is just as though a skillful butcher or his apprentice cut it with a carving knife lord i cannot bear lord the fever of my body is just as though a couple of lusty fellows had seized a weakling by his limbs and toasted him and roasted him over a fire pit law and i cannot bear it etc etc and there are signs of the pains growing worse so the exalted 1 instructed him browsed him gladdened him and comforted him with dharma talk then rose from his seat and departed now not long after the exalted 1's departure the venerable paguna died and at the time of his death his faculties were clear and calm then when the venus to the exalted 1 saluted him and sat down at 1 side so seated he said lord not long after the exalted 1 left the verbal kaguna died and at that time his faculties were clear and calm but why ananda should not the faculties of the mong paguna have been clear and calm the monk's mind and under had not been wholly freed from the 5 lower factors but when he heard that dhamma teaching his mind was wholly free there are 6 advantages ananda in hearing dhamma in time in testing its goodness in time what 6 consider consider ananda the monk whose mind is not wholly freed from the 5 lower factors but when dying is able to see the tatar gata the tatargata teaches him dhamma lovely in the beginning lovely in the middle lovely in the end its goodness its significance and also makes the holy life wholly fulfilled perfectly pure when he has heard that dhamma teaching his mind is wholly freed from the 5 lower fetus this ananda is the first advantage in hearing dhamma in time or though not just able to see the tata gather sees his disciple who teaches him dhamma and makes known the holy life etc then is his mind wholly free this ananda is the second advantage or though not able to see the tata gata or his disciple continues to reflect in mind on dhamma as heard as learned ponders over it draws over it then is his mind wholly free this ananda is the third advantage in testing his goodness in time consider ananda the monk whose mind is wholly freed from the 5 lower fetters whose mind is not wholly freed in respect of the complete destruction of the root of becoming who when dying is able to see the tatar gather the tata teaches him dhamma makes known the holy life when he has heard that dhamma teaching his mind is wholly freed in respect of the complete destruction of the root of becoming this ananda is the fourth advantage in hearing dhamma in time or he sees that as disciple who teaches him dhamma etc then is this is his mind holy free this ananda is the fifth advantage all though not able to see the tata gata or his disciple ever reflects in mind on dhamma as heard as learned ponders on it pause over it as he does so his mind is wholly free as to the complete destruction of the root of becoming this ananda is the sixth advantage in testing his goodness in time thoroughly and undone these are the 6 advantages in hearing dharma in time and testing its goodness in time it's the end of the suta
commentary: so in this utah we find that this vulnerable paguna he was suffering so much that he see the way he described not the pains in his body you know you can imagine the pain that he suffered and the the in the sutas the buddha said that and even an arya has to suffer bodily pains a putujana suffers both bodily pains as well as mental pains but an arya does not suffer mental pains because he accepts things as they are as they come because he knows life anitra everything changes but still not like this monkey he suffered so much uh bodily pain and then when he died the buddha said that initially he had not been wholly freed from the 5 lower factors that means he was not yet [Music] anagami he could have been sotapanna or sakadagamin but after hearing the buddha's teaching his mind was wholly freed from the 5 lower factors 5 lower factors meaning that he is an or at least an anagami [Music] now earlier we read that arya and in and even is obtained in during the 5 occasions from hearing dhamma teaching dhamma repeating dhamma reflecting on dhamma and during meditation and this uttara again confirms that aryah or arahana can be obtained from listening to dhamma this is here the buddha said either that person hears the dhamma from the buddha himself and he obtains freedom from the 5 lower factors or if he is already free from the 5 lower factors and just from hearing dhamma itself he can become an arahant or if he's not able to hear it from the buddha himself then he hears it from a disciple of the buddha meaning he hears it from another monk or even if he does not hear it from another monk then he reflects on dhamma the dhamma the discourses of the buddha that he has heard when he reflects on it now uh it is possible that the 5 and lower factors can be destroyed or if they are already destroyed then he can even attain arahanth by reflecting on dhamma and the other 1 is listening to dhamma so this is 1 of the sutas that again confirms that by listening to suta or reflecting on dhamma [Music] then a person can attain aryah or arahan
aud_file: 059_Anguttara Nikaya Book 6E 6655 - 6657 by Bhante Hye Dhammavuddho Mahathera.mp3

items: The monk whose mind is not wholly freed from the 5 lower factors but when dying is able to see the Tathagata
The monk who sees his disciple who teaches him Dhamma and makes known the Holy Life
The monk who continues to reflect in mind on Dhamma as heard, learned, ponders over it, draws over it
The monk whose mind is wholly freed from the 5 lower fetters, whose mind is not wholly freed in respect of the complete destruction of the root of becoming
All though not able to see the Tathagata or his disciple, ever reflects in mind on Dhamma as heard, learned, ponders on it, pauses over it
The monk who has seen the Tathagata and is able to see him

commentary: this is 1 of the well known sutas and it's also mentioned in the vinaya about this monk this month is called sona kolivisa and he was supposed to come to come from a very rich family and was so uh so delicate that it seemed that the book at the source of his feet and at the bottom of his feet now hair grew and uh it was that is what is stated in the books and after he became a monk he was very diligent in practicing and he tried not to sleep at night and he whenever he was tired or sleepy he would walk and he walked so much that his delicate feet broke man sort of was cut and he bled and he bled so much and that in the morning when they look at the place where he walked meditation uh he was so um bloodstained that they thought he was like somebody had just slaughtered a cow or something there and in spite of all his hard work he was not yet an arahant so because of putting too much energy he was he lost heart and he thought of this robin so the buddha came to him and told him that as a layman he used to play the guitar so the buddha said if the guitar strings were too tight it would not sound well and it was too loose also would not sound loud but it was tuned to the middle picture then it would sound very well so the buddha advised him to practice the middle path and he did so and soon enough he became an arahant and when he became an arahant like those other arahanza they don't come and say that i have become an arahant they just declare knowledge by saying the how uh arahanna sees things as they really are now the next 2 thai

commentary: so the last part here is about dukkha or suffering to carbon can be translated as suffering or sorrow or unsatisfactoriness etc now here dukkha is generally defined as being born as dukkha aging is too kasich dying is dukkha sorrow lamentation pain grief despair dukkha not to get what 1 wants is to cut in short the 5 aggregates of clinging is dukkha the 5 aggregate of clinging is basically body and mind the mind part consists of 4 things feelings perception volition and consciousness so these 4 things plus body makes up the 5 aggregates of clinging these are the 5 things that we all cling to and which we all consider to be the self whenever you refer to yourself in the past life or in the future life or in the present life you always refer to yourself in connection with these 5 things these 5 aggregates body feelings perception volition consciousness these 5 things and it is because of clinging to these 5 things that suffering arises now there are some ducat that is above measure that is very extreme very great then some ducati is trifling some dukkha that is quick to change and some dukkha that is slow to change and then when 1 is overcome by dukkha 1 grieves mourns laments beats his breast and becomes wielded or roams about in search of 1 who knows a spell or 2 to end the dukkah dukkha yields bewilderment and search i say now this dukkha yields bewilderment and search it is because of this result of dukkha that dukkha is a very good teacher if not for dukkha we would not want to walk this spiritual path it is because as we go through life and we suffer then we get bewildered and then we search for a way to end our dukkha and if we are sincere enough we will come across the right dhamma and that will lead us out of suffering is the best teacher in the world if you if a person doesn't has not had enough of dukkha then if you try to teach him the spiritual path he would not be interested it is only at a certain time when a person is spiritually mature through suffering then that person is interested to cultivate the holy life to to endure [Music]

sutta: now when the night was well advanced a deva shedding rays of far-reaching loveliness over jetta grove visited the exalted 1 saluted and stood at 1 side and so standing he spoke thus to the exalted 1 lord there are these 6 things that lead not to amongst falling away what 6 reverence for teacher reverence for dhamma reverence for the sangha reverence for the training meekness and having good friends lord these 6 things lead not to amongst falling away thus spoke that deva and the teacher approved and that they were perceiving that the master agreed saluted and disappeared thence keeping the exalted 1 on his right now at the at the end of that night the exalted 1 addressed the monks and told them all that had passed and we had when when he had spoken the wearable sariputta saluted the exalted 1 and said lord the meaning of the exalted ones brief words i thus understand in full suppose lord among himself reveres the teacher and praises such reverence he will instill such reverence in others who like it and of those who possess it he will speak in praise justly truly and timely so too of reverence for the dhamma reverence for this sangha reverence for the training meekness and having good friends it is thus i understand in full the exalted 1's brief words well said well said sariputta it is just as you say and sarikuta thus the full meaning of my brief words ought to be understood as the end of the sutta
commentary: so in this suta these 6 things are important for among to have veneration for the teacher the buddha veneration for dhamma and veneration water sangha veneration for the training the monks training includes the precepts and other things that among must learn and beingness weakness is a quality um among should possess love not to be aggressive to be mean and gentle and having good friends having good friends friends good friends satoshi who are knowledgeable in the dhamma or interested in the dhamma and who practice the holy life because as we practice the holy life with good friends now we support each other encourage each other and that is important because there are times when a person may get discouraged especially a person who's new on the holy path okay the next suta
aud_file: 061_Anguttara Nikaya Book 6G 6663 - 6878 by Bhante Hye Dhammavuddho Mahathera.mp3

sutta: monks if a monk follow 6 things he will live here and now in great happiness and contentment and for him the mole has begun to form for destroying the asavas what 6 hearing among delights in dhamma in development bhavana in renunciation or abandoning in total seclusion in being free of ill will and in non-proliferation of thoughts monks if a follow if a monk follow these 6 things he will live in great happiness here and now and contentment and for him the mold has begun to form for destroying the ask of us as the end of the suta
commentary: destroying the asavas means becoming an arahant and these 6 things are important he delights in dhamma that means he loves the dhamma loves to study or listen to dhamma and discuss dhamma in development bhavana development means development of the mind and that means practicing meditation and especially attaining samadhi because when a person attains samadhi the hindrances are abandoned third 1 is renunciation abandoning letting go fourth 1 is total seclusion total exclusion or aloofness being aloof from others and then being free of ill will not to have anger and the last 1 is not non-proliferation of thoughts for most people there is always a proliferation of thoughts 1 thought becomes 10 10 becomes a 100 etc and non-proliferation of thoughts can be attained when we attain 1 pointedness of mind when we attain the jhanas then we experience a bliss happiness free of thinking and that happiness which is free of thinking surpasses the happiness of thinking it is only true then that we can renounce thinking

items: being free of ill will and non-proliferation of thoughts
